note: Canonical results from the published cross-system run. Judge = gpt-4o-mini (neutral, ground-truth-blind). n=20, native configs. Source: inspeximus/probes/integrity_bench_{revert,echo}.py in the Agora repo; write-up 'We fixed our own memory benchmark until it stopped flattering us'.

inspeximus:revert.note: intent router restores the predecessor from the version ledger

mem0:revert.note: no revert operation; the corrected value is retained

graphiti:revert.note: no revert operation; bitemporal invalidation fires on named contradictions, not an unnamed 'go back'

inspeximus:echo.note: echo_guard blocks the restated stale value

reading: Value-obscuring revert is a capability gap: among the systems tested here, only inspeximus exposes a first-class channel to undo a correction on an unnamed command, and the inspeximus/mem0 CIs do not overlap. Echo resurrection is a tie. The revert gap is judge-independent because mem0 and Graphiti structurally lack the operation; the judge model only shifts inspeximus's absolute rate. (Revert is rare, not unique: Letta ships an engine-level checkpoint-undo, not tested here.)
